text:
In a small town nestled between forests and the sea lived Jack. Jack loved collecting acorns. Each one felt special. One crisp afternoon, a big wind blew through town. Trees fell with an oomph. Many acorns were carried far away.

Jack searched everywhere. He visited distant forests. He even sent letters asking people far across the land.

After many moons, a small fox sniffed out an acorn somewhere deep in the forest. It reminded him of home. That night fox visited Jack again. You see, foxes and owls liked acorns. They made little friendship homes sometimes.

Jack got his acorns back years later. They came from an owl. That same night owl flew away singing a catchy little tune—

Ah'owl Ow Ow—

Jack loved that song. It felt like a special friendship too.

From then on every autumn, Jack would whistle the owl song. And somewhere deep in the forest loved an owl song visitor.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
